rawContent: {"stage":"review","insights":[{"kind":"gotcha","text":"Creating services per request (e.g. `createNotificationService()` in route handlers) resets in-memory state and causes data loss across requests.","scope":"src/services"},{"kind":"lesson","text":"A singleton store module-level (`let store = ...`) is initialized once at load time; if that store is later replaced by reassignment inside a factory function, callers still hold the old reference and","scope":"src/services"},{"kind":"convention","text":"Prefer singleton class instances (shared `static` field) over module-level `let` variables for shared mutable state — the class field is stable and won't be shadowed.","scope":"src/services"}]}
